subject: Sports
title: The Laws of the Game
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Laws of the Game usable.

hook.text: Football runs on just 17 laws. Simplicity is what makes the game universal.

buildup.text: The Laws of the Game are maintained by IFAB—not FIFA—making them independent of any one organization.

discovery.text: A match needs a ball, two goals, a pitch, and two teams. That's it. No expensive equipment required.

twist.text: Red and yellow cards didn't exist until 1970. A referee invented them after language barriers caused confusion.

climax.text: The simplicity of football's rules is the reason it's played in every country on Earth.

punchline.text: Seventeen rules govern the most popular game in history.
